task_completion.description: Shapes for the royal family members are created in the draw.io canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for multiple shapes (around 25) representing the royal family members in the draw.io workspace.

task_completion.description: Member shapes are labeled with both their Name and Birth Year.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the text inside the shapes. It must include the name and the birth year (e.g., 'Queen Elizabeth II\nb. 1926').

task_completion.description: The diagram is arranged in a hierarchical layout spanning 4 generations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the diagram structure. It should have a clear top-down or tree-like layout with 4 distinct levels/generations, starting with Queen Elizabeth II and Prince Philip at the top.

task_completion.description: Solid lines are used for parent-child relationships and dashed lines for spouse relationships.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the connecting lines between shapes. Lines connecting spouses (e.g., Elizabeth and Philip) must be dashed. Lines connecting parents to children must be solid.

task_completion.description: The draw.io project contains a 'Family Tree' page and a 'Legend' page explaining the line styles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom of the draw.io workspace for page tabs named 'Family Tree' and 'Legend'. If the Legend page is visible, it should contain text explaining that solid lines mean parent-child and dashed lines mean spouse.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as royal_family_tree.drawio and exported as royal_family_tree.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Desktop background, a file manager window, or terminal output to confirm the presence of both 'royal_family_tree.drawio' and 'royal_family_tree.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io application open and being actively used, either by manually adding shapes or using the CSV import feature.

integrity.description: The agent exported the PNG using the draw.io GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the 'File -> Export as -> PNG' menu or export dialogs in draw.io, rather than terminal commands for conversion.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided CSV data to populate the family tree.
integrity.visual_evidence: The names and birth years in the diagram should match the British Royal Family (e.g., Queen Elizabeth II, King Charles III), not placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The family tree must contain 25 members across 4 generations. Root ancestors (Gen 1) are Queen Elizabeth II (b. 1926) and Prince Philip (b. 1921). Gen 4 includes great-grandchildren like Prince George (b. 2013) and Princess Lilibet (b. 2021). The diagram should have 30 solid parent-child edges and 14 dashed spouse edges. The draw.io file must be named 'royal_family_tree.drawio' and the exported image 'royal_family_tree.png'.
